title: BDLS Exam
description: Basic Disaster Life Support exam questions

questions.text: What is all-hazards preparedness?
options: Process of identifying at-risk populations for a disaster or public health emergency
Determination if needs are greater than resources
Uniform approach to prepare and respond to hazard regardless of cause
Unique approach to respond to various types of natural disasters
questions.explanation: Uniform approach to prepare and respond to hazard regardless of cause
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: Which of the following is the most appropriate authority to contact if an incident of bioterrorism is suspected?
options: Local or state public health department
questions.explanation: Local or state public health department
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: What is a blast injury?
options: Injury resulting from an explosive device
questions.explanation: Injury resulting from an explosive device
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: What is one essential component of a regional disaster plan?
options: Consider all types of natural disasters
Rely on FEMA or other federal authorities
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: In a scene with hazardous materials, in what zone would decontamination occur?
options: None of these answers are correct
questions.subject: Environmental and Toxicology

questions.text: How should a responder prepare for deployment to a disaster zone?
options: All of these answers are strategies to prepare for deployment
questions.explanation: All of these answers are strategies to prepare for deployment
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: What is one tool to help diagnose nerve agents?
questions.subject: Environmental and Toxicology

questions.text: What is the most common disaster worldwide?
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: Which statement is true about disaster triage?
options: Transport all casualties to nearest hospital quickly
Provide the greatest good for each individual patient
questions.subject: Patient Assessment

questions.text: What is the leading environmental cause of death in the United States?
questions.subject: Environmental and Toxicology

questions.text: What is the first step of SALT mass casualty triage?
questions.subject: Patient Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following is a function of the public health system during a disaster?
options: Protect population against injury and disease
Transport casualties to local health care centers
None of these answers are functions of the public health system
questions.explanation: Protect population against injury and disease
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: Which of the following best defines the function of public health?
options: To recognize health threats that may affect communities
To provide emergency care and transport sick and injured to medical facility
To ensure scene safety and security
To prevent, delay, and treat disease, injury, and disability in individual patients
questions.explanation: To recognize health threats that may affect communities
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: What is one challenge of Incident Management?
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: What is the initial goal of mass casualty triage?
options: Ensure that all patients are treated equally
Remove the dead from the scene
Treat all patients, regardless of injury, as quickly as possible
Sort and assess casualties, identifying life-threatening injuries
questions.explanation: Sort and assess casualties, identifying life-threatening injuries
questions.subject: Patient Assessment

questions.text: What type of injury is least common after a nuclear or radiological disaster?
questions.subject: Environmental and Toxicology

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a mental health impact of disaster?
questions.subject: Medical/Behavioral/Geriatric

questions.text: What is a nuclear event?
options: Release of radioactive materials to human-populated area
Nuclear detonation and accompanying massive explosion
questions.explanation: Nuclear detonation and accompanying massive explosion
questions.subject: Environmental and Toxicology

questions.text: What is the first priority of all citizens or responders?
options: Be able to help in a crisis
Be ready to immediately deploy to disaster area
questions.subject: Patient Safety

questions.text: Which of the following is a clue that a chemical disaster is probable?
options: Known hazard, substance leaking or spilling
All of these answers are clues of a chemical disaster
Multiple 911 calls from the same area
questions.explanation: All of these answers are clues of a chemical disaster
questions.subject: Environmental and Toxicology

questions.text: Which activity should take place as part of the recovery from a disaster or public health emergency?
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: What does resilience in the PRE-DISASTER Paradigm refer to?
options: The ability of the federal government to return things to normal
The ability of a community to prevent disasters
The ability of a community or a person to return to normal after an event
questions.explanation: The ability of a community or a person to return to normal after an event
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: What is a component of a risk analysis?
options: Do the greatest good for the greatest number
Ability to overcome adversity due to a disaster
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: When does treatment at the disaster scene end?
options: After all casualties transported from scene
questions.explanation: After all casualties transported from scene
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: What sort of treatment may be performed prior to decontamination?
options: Treatment not requiring personal protective equipment
questions.subject: Environmental and Toxicology

questions.text: What triage category would you assign to a casualty that requires care that can be safely delayed without affecting probability of survival?
questions.subject: Patient Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a vulnerable population in a disaster or other public health emergency?
options: All of these answers are examples of a vulnerable population
questions.explanation: All of these answers are examples of a vulnerable population
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: Which of the following is not a way that a chemical may enter the body?
questions.subject: Environmental and Toxicology

questions.text: What is a potential illness or injury that would occur weeks after a natural disaster?
questions.subject: Disaster

questions.text: Which of the following account for most terror events worldwide?
questions.subject: Disaster
